title: Mastering Command Arguments
description: Learn how to handle, validate, and use command arguments like a pro, including players, numbers, booleans, and advanced parsing.

lessons.title: Argument Basics
lessons.content:
# What Are Arguments?

Arguments are extra words after a command.

Example:
`/heal Steve`

- args[0] = "Steve"

## Example Code
```java
if (args.length > 0) {
    sender.sendMessage("Hello " + args[0]);
}
```

## Logic
- args is an array
- Starts at index 0

## Edge Case
Accessing args[0] without checking length will crash.

quiz.question: What happens if args empty and accessed?

lessons.title: Validating Arguments
Player Arguments
Number Arguments
Boolean Arguments
Multiple Arguments & Subcommands
lessons.content:
# Multiple Args

## Example
`/give Steve diamond 5`

```java
if (args.length < 3) return true;

String playerName = args[0];
String item = args[1];
int amount = Integer.parseInt(args[2]);
```

## Logic
- Each index = different meaning

## Subcommands
```java
if (args[0].equalsIgnoreCase("give")) {}
```

## Structure
- args[0] = action
- args[1+] = data

quiz.question: What is args[0] often used for?

lessons.title: Advanced Parsing & Edge Cases
lessons.content:
# Advanced Handling

## Join Arguments
```java
String message = String.join(" ", args);
```

## Example
`/say Hello world`

## Logic
- Combine all args

## Edge Cases
- Empty args
- Invalid types
- Offline players

## Best Practice
Validate EVERYTHING before use.
